data.exercise_block: Pouvoir (can, may) expresses ability and capability.
Pouvoir (can, may) expresses ability and capability.
Pouvoir (can, may) expresses ability and capability.
When asking permission with inversion in formal style, the first-person singular of pouvoir takes a different form.
When asking permission with inversion in formal style, the first-person singular of pouvoir takes a different form.
Another formal way of asking questions is to use the conditional form of pouvoir. We'll cover this in more depth in Chapter 12.
Another formal way of asking questions is to use the conditional form of pouvoir. We'll cover this in more depth in Chapter 12.
The verb vouloir (to want) is used to express wishes and desires. It is also used for a polite request in the conditional form.

data.exercise_block: Proniminal verbs in the passé composé
data.question_text: As we mentioned earlier, all pronominal verbs are conjugated with être. The reflexive pronouns precede the auxiliary verb (être). In most cases, the past participle agrees in gender and number with the subject of the pronominal verb.
Elle s'est promenée sur la plage.
Ils se sont ennuyés à la réception.
Il s'est évanoui à cause de la chaleur.
In the negative, the negation is placed around the auxiliary verb être.
Ils ne se sont pas couchés de bonne heure.
Elle ne s'est pas promenée le long du canal.
In the interrogative form, the reflexive pronoun is placed before être.
S'est-il rendu compte de son erreur?
Vous êtes-vous bien amusés à la fête?
Note that the past participle does not agree with the subject of the pronominal verb when the verb is followed by a direct object or by another verb.
Elle s'est offert une nouvelle moto.
Elle s'est fait arracher une dent.
When reciprocal verbs take a direct object, the past participle agrees with the subject.
Ils se sont rencontrés à Venise.
Ils se sont mariés en mars.
